title: ROCK VARNISH GEOCHEMICAL PROVENANCE ANALYSIS
what: Steatite Sourcing — XRD/XRF investigation of Fe/Mn stratigraphic layering in rock varnish; traced 10+ artifacts to Rhode Island quarries via d-spacing crystallographic fingerprinting
Ceramic Provenance — d-spacing fingerprinting revealing regional clay sources and trade patterns across North Fork, South Fork, and Oyster Island sources
Multi-institutional collaboration (BNL + Stony Brook University Anthropology); Late Holocene indigenous material sourcing spanning Terminal Archaic through Late Woodland periods (3,500–2,400 B.P.)
how: XRD: 2θ = 20–75° sweeps characterizing crystal structure and mineral phases for both steatite rock varnish and ceramic clay mineralogy
XRF: Elemental mapping of Fe, Mn, and trace elements across 10+ samples for both material types; d-spacing fingerprinting across 2.43–3.81 Å range to establish unique sourcing signatures

Sample preparation involved surface cleaning, thin-section mounting, and powder preparation for XRD/XRF analysis — prepared samples shown are representative of typical surface treatment and mounting protocol
results: Steatite: 6 samples with Fe/Mn stratigraphic layering consistent with Younger Dryas formation; 10+ artifacts traced to Rhode Island quarries

Ceramics: elemental distribution mapping (example shown) revealed 10–45% variation across regional sources, with distinct geochemical signatures separating North Fork, South Fork, and Oyster Island clay sources
Confirmed long-distance trade networks (100+ miles); published in NYSAA Bulletin Vol. 138, 2024 and BNL technical report
